prose:
Frontier-orbital energies of ethylene

Method provenance
• Property: HOMO, LUMO energies and HOMO–LUMO gap (eV)
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting)
• Geometry provenance: single-point on supplied fixed Cartesian geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz); no re-optimization (DFT//input).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet).
• Software: PySCF (via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill).
• SCF convergence: converged in 6 cycles.
• Warnings: no warnings reported.
• Live log path (written during run): /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp6acc_31u/frontier_*.out

Results (eV)
HOMO-2  −11.487503
HOMO-1  −9.883854
HOMO    −7.6155818
LUMO    −0.17986527
LUMO+1  +1.720103
LUMO+2  +2.3802006

HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans) = 7.4357165 eV
Vertical ionization potential (−ε_HOMO) = 7.6155818 eV
Vertical electron affinity (−ε_LUMO) = 0.17986527 eV

All values were computed with the chemkit “frontier-orbitals” task at B3LYP/def2-TZVP in the gas phase; engine integrity verdict: trustworthy.
